task_completion.description: Open a terminal or Docker management GUI to interact with Docker.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshot shows a terminal window open or the Docker Desktop application launched.
Screenshot shows the output of a command like 'docker ps' or the Docker Desktop GUI displaying 'test-web-server' as a currently running container.

task_completion.description: Execute the action to stop the target container.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshot shows the command 'docker stop test-web-server' being executed in the terminal, or the cursor clicking the 'Stop' button next to the container in a GUI.

task_completion.description: Check the list of all containers to ensure 'test-web-server' has not been deleted.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshot shows the output of 'docker ps -a' or the Docker Desktop GUI where 'test-web-server' is still visible in the list of all containers after the stop action.

task_completion.description: Confirm the status of 'test-web-server' is stopped.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshot shows the status of 'test-web-server' explicitly listed as 'Exited' (in terminal output) or 'Stopped' (in GUI).

integrity.description: Verify the agent is interacting with a real terminal or Docker GUI.
integrity.visual_evidence: Commands and outputs should appear in a genuine terminal emulator or Docker Desktop application, not mocked in a text editor or document.

integrity.description: Verify the agent targets the pre-existing container rather than creating a fake one.
integrity.visual_evidence: The container being stopped must be exactly named 'test-web-server' and should ideally show 'nginx:alpine' as its image or port 8080 if visible.

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not delete the container.
integrity.visual_evidence: There should be no execution of 'docker rm test-web-server' in the terminal, nor any clicking of a delete/trash icon in the GUI.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target container is exactly named 'test-web-server' and was created using the 'nginx:alpine' image. The successful end state requires this specific container to be in an 'Exited' or 'Stopped' state but still present in the system's container list (it must not be removed).
